name: Laguna de las Doblas — circular by Sanlúcar la Mayor
name_original: Laguna de las Doblas — circular por Sanlúcar la Mayor
region_intro: Hiking around Sanlúcar La Mayor offers routes through a landscape characterized by river plains, dehesa woodlands, and lagoon areas. The region features gentle elevations, with trails often following natural watercourses like the Guadiamar River. This area provides a mix of open terrain and shaded paths, suitable for various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Sanlúcar La Mayor The most popular hiking route is Laguna de las Doblas — circular by Sanlúcar la Mayor, a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete, offering views of the local lagoon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aznalcóllar Green Corridor Loop, a difficult 13.8 miles (22.3 km) path. This long distance trail follows a green corridor, providing varied terrain over several hours. Local hikers also love the Laguna de las Doblas – Dehesa of the Guadiamar loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through dehesa woodlands and along the Guadiamar river, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Sanlúcar La Mayor is defined by river paths, dehesa woodlands, and lagoon areas. The network offers options for easy hikes Sanlúcar La Mayor, as well as moderate and difficult routes for different ability levels. The routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Sanlúcar La Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Sanlúcar La Mayor offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 15 easy, 25 moderate, and 1 difficult trail, ensuring options for everyone from beginners to experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect on the hikes around Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The hiking trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor traverse a varied landscape, primarily characterized by river plains, expansive dehesa woodlands, and serene lagoon areas. You'll find gentle elevations and paths that often follow natural watercourses like the Guadiamar River, offering a mix of open terrain and shaded sections.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Sanlúcar La Mayor has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or families. A great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010568716/laguna-de-las-doblas-circular-by-sanlucar-la-mayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas — circular by Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.8-mile (4.6 km) trail that takes just over an hour to complete, offering pleasant views of the local lagoon.

Q: Where can I find more challenging long-distance hikes?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010832581/aznalcollar-green-corridor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aznalcóllar Green Corridor Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.8-mile (22.3 km) path. This route follows a green corridor, providing varied terrain and a longer duration for experienced hikers.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19348515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas – Dehesa of the Guadiamar loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.4-mile (10.3 km) trail that explores dehesa woodlands and the Guadiamar river.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting natural features and landmarks. You might encounter the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For scenic views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3113504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent panoramas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts historical points of interest. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1606627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River&lt;/a&gt;, or explore religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7086624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Loreto&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1588085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Cuatrovitas&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible from nearby routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the peaceful river paths, the beauty of the dehesa woodlands, and the serene lagoon areas, highlighting the region's natural charm and diverse scenery.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most public hiking trails in Spain, including those around Sanlúcar La Mayor. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in natural parks or areas with wildlife, and to clean up after them to preserve the environment for everyone.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, you can find amenities near some of the hiking routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2858510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva&lt;/a&gt; is a convenient spot to grab a refreshment or a meal, often located close to popular trailheads or recreation areas.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many popular starting points for hikes in Sanlúcar La Mayor, especially those near natural recreation areas like Las Doblas, typically offer designated parking facilities. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The best time to hike in Sanlúcar La Mayor is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. Spring also brings blooming wildflowers, enhancing the scenic beauty.
tips: Path that is close to the riverbed of the Río Guadiamar and where you can find animals and different species associated with the river! Quiet road to walk on!

This recreational area is located next to the Las Doblas bridge in the municipality of Sanlúcar la Mayor. In this place, an artificial lagoon has been created in what was an old gravel pit, whose surroundings can be explored through a wooden walkway.
